title: 10 best herbal creams for hot flashes

remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian cream helps to reduce anxiety and stress associated with hot flashes, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Pain relief: Valerian contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate hot flash-related discomfort, such as breast tenderness, headaches, and joint pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve circulation, reducing the frequency and intensity of hot flashes by regulating body temperature and preventing overheating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with hot flashes because they contain valerenic acid, a key compound that interacts with the brain's neurotransmitters to regulate body temperature. The cream's cooling effect is said to provide relief from the intense heat associated with hot flashes. Additionally, valerian's natural sedative properties may also help to calm the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to hot flashes. This holistic approach can provide much-needed relief for women experiencing this common menopausal symptom.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with hot flashes by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to hot flash episodes.
Isovaleric acid: It helps with hot flashes by having a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, which can alleviate the emotional and psychological symptoms associated with hot flashes.

Valeranone: It helps with hot flashes by having a sedative and anti-stress effect, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are widely used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which helps to reduce hot flashes and promote relaxation.
Roots: Valerian roots also contain valepotriates, which have anti-anxiety and sedative properties, making them a popular choice for hot flash relief creams.
Roots: Valerian root extracts are also known for their ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to hot flashes, making them a key ingredient in many creams.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots in 100 milliliters of vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine the extracted liquid with 100 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds intervals until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Estrogenic Properties: Herbal angelica cream contains phytoestrogens, which can help alleviate hot flashes by binding to estrogen receptors, regulating hormonal imbalances, and reducing vasomotor symptoms.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, may reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash-related discomforts like sweating, flushing, and itching.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in angelica cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica, also known as angelica, creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural phytoestrogenic properties. It can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which helps to regulate hormonal imbalances that cause hot flashes. The herb contains compounds such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, which have been shown to have a cooling effect on the body and may help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. This makes angelica creams a popular natural remedy for menopausal symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Angelica archangelica creams may help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, sinapic acid): Phenolic acids in Angelica archangelica may help mitigate hot flashes by possessing antioxidant properties, which can help regulate body temperature and reduce oxidative stress.

Coumarins (e.g., scopoletin, umbelliferone): Coumarins in Angelica archangelica creams may help alleviate hot flashes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help regulate blood flow and reduce heat sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are commonly used due to their high content of angelic acid and isopimpinellin, which have been shown to have a cooling effect and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain a higher amount of essential oils compared to other parts, which helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the occurrence of hot flashes.

Stems: Stems are utilized as they have a high concentration of angelica lactone, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of angelica archangelica roots and leaves on a dry morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steep 20g of dried angelica archangelica in 200ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make a decoction.
Strain the decoction and mix it with 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the ingredients have fully incorporated and thickened.

remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to reduce hot flash frequency and severity by calming the nervous system and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to hot flashes.
Valerian root: Has a sedative effect that helps to regulate body temperature and promote relaxation, reducing the discomfort associated with hot flashes.

Dong quai: Is used in traditional Chinese medicine to balance the body's energy (qi) and alleviate symptoms such as hot flashes, night sweats, and vaginal dryness by promoting hormonal balance and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with hot flashes because of its unique ability to regulate the body's temperature and hormone balance. The herbal extract is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger hot flashes. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata creams contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the skin and promote relaxation, providing relief from hot flash symptoms and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have a calming effect on the body, which may help alleviate hot flashes by reducing stress and anxiety.
Alkaloids (specifically, harmine and harmaline): These alkaloids have a sedative effect, which may help regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
Isoflavones (specifically, vitexin and orientin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the symptoms of hot flashes, such as sweating and flushing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to have a positive effect on hot flashes.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for hot flash relief.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, including flavonol glycosides, which may help reduce hot flashes by alleviating hormonal imbalances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves by cutting them with scissors during early morning hours.
Combine the harvested flowers and leaves with 500ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 30 minutes then strain the mixture using a cheesecloth to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 200g of unsalted butter and 200ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius.
Whisk the mixture continuously for 10 minutes then store it in an airtight container at room temperature for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing sweating and discomfort.

Hypericin: As an antioxidant, hypericin helps reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hot flashes, and may also help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes.

Flavonoids and Phenolic Acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of hot flashes such as anxiety, sweating, and rapid heartbeat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with hot flashes because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's cooling and astringent effects can provide quick relief from hot flashes, while its emollient properties can help to moisturize and protect the skin, leaving it feeling calm and comforted. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for managing hot flashes.
remedy_constituents: Hypericin: A naphthodianthrone with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce hot flashes by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation associated with menopause.
Hypaconitine: A phenolic compound with estrogenic and neuroprotective effects, which can help alleviate hot flashes by modulating estrogen levels and reducing symptoms of menopause.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce hot flashes by decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, particularly hyperoside, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: They are a primary source of hyperforin, a key compound with vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate hot flashes.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids and glycosides, including hyperoside, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and help regulate body temperature.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom stage for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers at 30°c for 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine dried flowers with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and fill 50g jars with the herbal cream for storage.

remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Oats contain a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation, providing relief from hot flash-related sweating and discomfort.

Antioxidant Properties: Oat extracts are rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms, promoting overall skin health and well-being.

Moisturizing Properties: The emollients present in oat creams help to lock in moisture and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by hot flashes and providing a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, creams helps with hot flashes because of its unique ability to regulate hormonal balance and promote relaxation. The calming properties of Avena sativa help to soothe the nervous system, reducing the severity and frequency of hot flashes. Additionally, Avena sativa has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as sweating and palpitations. This makes Avena sativa creams a popular natural remedy for managing hot flashes.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: Avenacoside, a unique saponin glycoside in Avena sativa, helps alleviate hot flashes by modulating estrogen receptors and providing antioxidant effects, which can reduce menopausal symptoms.

Isovitexin: Isovitexin, a lignan glycoside, has been shown to have estrogenic activity and antioxidant properties, contributing to the reduction of hot flashes by regulating hormonal balance and protecting against oxidative stress.

Fatty acids and sterols: The presence of fatty acids and sterols, such as unsaturated fatty acids and beta-sitosterol, in Avena sativa creams may help to regulate hormone levels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in isoleucine, an amino acid that helps to regulate blood flow and alleviate hot flashes.
Roots: They contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce hot flashes.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that may help to alleviate hot flashes by regulating estrogen levels.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried avena sativa in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a liquid extract.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to separate the solids from the liquid.
Combine 1/4 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of vegetable oil in a small saucepan and heat gently.
Add 2 tablespoons of the avena sativa liquid extract to the saucepan and mix well for 30 seconds.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating to set the cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Tilia extracts contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash symptoms by neutralizing free radicals.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The essential oils present in Tilia flowers have been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, which may help calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and discomfort associated with hot flashes.

Hormone-balancing activity: Some studies suggest that Tilia extracts may have a mild effect on hormone regulation, potentially helping to balance estrogen levels and alleviate symptoms related to menopause, including hot flashes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia platyphyllos, also known as broad-leaved lime, creams helps with hot flashes because of its calming and regulating properties. The cream's active compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to balance hormonal fluctuations that trigger hot flashes. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Tilia platyphyllos also contribute to its efficacy in soothing and cooling the skin, providing relief from hot flash symptoms. Regular use of these creams may help alleviate symptoms and promote a more stable body temperature.
remedy_constituents: Tiliaflavonoids: These flavonoids have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by regulating body temperature and hormone levels.

Terpenoids (specifically, Linalool): As a natural anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory agent, Linalool helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with hot flashes, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness.

Flavonols (specifically, Quercetin): Quercetin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hot flashes, promoting overall hormonal balance and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties, which help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Buds: They are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce hot flash severity.
Flowers: They are used due to their estrogenic properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances contributing to hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers in mid to late summer when in full bloom.
Dry tilia platyphyllos flowers in a warm dry place with good air circulation.
Infuse dried tilia platyphyllos flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil to remove solids and obtain clear liquid.
Mix the herbal infused oil with beeswax and other ingredients to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with hot flashes.
GABA-ergic activity: The GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) present in lemon balm cream helps to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate anxiety and restlessness caused by hot flashes.
Hormone-balancing effects: Lemon balm's estrogen-like properties help to balance hormone levels, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes experienced during menopause.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that have a calming effect on the body. The herb's sedative properties help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hot flashes. Additionally, Melissa officinalis creams have been shown to help regulate body temperature, providing relief from intense heat and flushing associated with hot flashes. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to manage hot flashes without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid compound that helps alleviate hot flashes by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help stabilize blood vessels and regulate body temperature.
Linalool: A terpene with a sedative and anxiolytic effect that can help reduce hot flashes by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and stabilizing hormone levels.

Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hot flash symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a cooling effect and help alleviate hot flashes.
Flowers: They contain estrogen-like compounds that can help stabilize hormonal fluctuations and reduce symptoms of hot flashes.
Stems: They also contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is at its peak fragrance and potency.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Infuse dried melissa officinalis in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil 1:5 ratio for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add beeswax and emulsify the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce hot flash frequency and severity by inhibiting the production of luteinizing hormone, which regulates estrogen levels and can contribute to hot flashes.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body that can exacerbate hot flashes and other menopausal symptoms.
Saponins: Have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to regulate body temperature and reduce sweating associated with hot flashes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with hot flashes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin, help to regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, licorice creams have been shown to improve mood and reduce anxiety, which can be contributing factors to hot flashes. The soothing and calming effects of licorice creams also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, further alleviating hot flash symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Licorinic acid glycosides: These constituents may help alleviate hot flashes by stabilizing cortisol levels and reducing the production of androgen hormones, which can contribute to hot flashes in some women.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin may help mitigate hot flashes by reducing inflammation, modulating the body's response to stress, and maintaining hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds may contribute to hot flash relief by scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the body's thermal regulation, leading to a decrease in hot flash frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin is extracted from the roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra, which is used to make creams for hot flashes due to its estrogen-mimicking properties.
Leaves: Licorice extract, which contains flavonoids, is derived from the leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra, used in creams for hot flashes to provide relief from symptoms.
Barks: Glycosides present in the barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in creams to alleviate hot flashes by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio for 2 hours.
Filter the extract to obtain a clear liquid and discard the solids then store it in an airtight container.
Mix 300g of the extract with 700g of a base oil like sweet almond oil in a stainless steel container.
Heat the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes then let it cool to 40°c and add 100g of beeswax.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has thickened to a creamy consistency then fill into jars.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate hot flash symptoms such as redness, swelling, and discomfort.

Estrogen-mimicking properties: Nettle contains estrogen-like compounds called isoflavones, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes, providing relief from sudden temperature changes and other symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in stinging nettle creams, such as histamine, may help dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and promoting a sense of coolness and relaxation, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms like sweating and flushing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with hot flashes because of its unique properties. The cream contains compounds that help regulate body temperature, reduce inflammation, and balance hormonal fluctuations associated with menopause. The antispasmodic and vasodilatory effects of Urtica dioica cream also help to relax blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments, providing relief and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin helps alleviate hot flashes by reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and improving blood vessel function.

Allicin: Although Urtica dioica does not contain allicin, it's a compound found in garlic. Urtica dioica cream may contain small amounts of allicin due to cross-contamination with garlic or it may be a by-product of the plant's volatile oils. However, studies have shown allicin helps reduce hot flashes by inhibiting the aromatase enzyme and improving estrogen levels in the body.
Tannins: Tannins in Urtica dioica help reduce hot flashes by stabilizing mast cell membranes, thereby preventing histamine release and subsequent vasodilation that can cause hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are commonly used due to their high concentration of isoorientin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of isoorientin and other compounds, which can help alleviate symptoms of hot flashes.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are used due to their high levels of saponins, compounds that can help regulate body temperature and reduce hot flash symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the urtica dioica leaves and stems on a sunny day when the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica leaves and stems in a cool dry place for 7 days.
Infuse the dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Mix the strained oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:1:1 then heat the mixture until melted.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon verbena cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress caused by hot flashes, which can lead to cellular damage and inflammation.
Aromatic Relaxation Response: The calming aroma of lemon verbena in the cream stimulates a relaxation response in the body, helping to reduce anxiety and tension associated with hot flashes.

Vasodilatory Effects: Lemon verbena's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes by allowing heat to dissipate more efficiently from the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, creams helps with hot flashes because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to balance hormones and regulate body temperature, providing relief from sudden heat surges. The creams also contain soothing and calming agents that ease anxiety and stress, common triggers of hot flashes. By addressing the root causes, Verbena officinalis creams offer a natural and holistic approach to managing hot flashes and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help alleviate hot flashes by exerting antioxidant and estrogenic effects, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms associated with menopause.

Rosmarinol: This phenolic compound may help alleviate hot flashes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to hot flashes.
Urospermaline: This phenolic compound may help alleviate hot flashes by exerting a mild estrogenic effect, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms associated with menopause.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: They are used due to their fragrant oils, which can help to regulate body temperature and reduce hot flashes.
Roots: They are used due to their content of volatile oils and iridoid glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various symptoms of menopause.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers on a sunny morning in late summer.
Infuse 250ml of organic glycerin in a double boiler with 25g of verbena officinalis for 30 minutes.
Combine 100g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Mix 50g of infused glycerin with the shea and coconut oil mixture to create a smooth cream base.
Pour the cream base into sterilized jars and seal them immediately for 6 weeks to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system, reducing the body's response to stress. The herb baicalin, found in skullcap, has a sedative effect that can help regulate bodily functions and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. By applying topical creams containing skullcap extract, women can experience a decrease in the frequency and intensity of these uncomfortable symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relief throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Scutellarein: A flavonoid compound found in skullcap that has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing vasodilation and sweating.

Baicalin: A flavonoid glycoside in skullcap that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hot flashes, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Acacetin: A flavone compound found in skullcap that has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for managing hot flash symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create creams that help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids, rhyzomes contribute to the development of creams that soothe and calm hot flashes, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried scutellaria baicalensis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered scutellaria baicalensis with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the scutellaria baicalensis and oil mixture and stir until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer until smooth and creamy.
Pour the scutellaria baicalensis cream into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that promote relaxation and calmness, thereby reducing symptoms of menopause. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the body, which helps to soothe and cool the skin. As a result, the cream provides instant relief from hot flashes by lowering body temperature and alleviating sweating, discomfort, and anxiety associated with menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hot flashes, providing relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and irritation.

Calming effects on the nervous system: The soothing scent of lavender has been shown to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate hot flashes, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Some studies suggest that lavender may help regulate estrogen levels, which can contribute to hot flash relief by normalizing hormonal fluctuations and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain compounds like linalyl acetate, which help to reduce the severity of hot flashes by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation.
Seeds: English lavender seeds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as skin irritation and sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100 grams of organic coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c to 80°c for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets and stir until fully incorporated, allowing the mixture to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 60 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of sweet almond oil with the cooled cream base and blend thoroughly until well combined, taking an additional 5 minutes to prepare.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, labeling each jar with the preparation date and contents, and storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hot flashes because of its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate this symptom. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile also help to alleviate redness and discomfort associated with hot flashes. Additionally, chamomile's natural cooling effect can provide immediate relief from heat and sweating, helping to regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to manage this common symptom of menopause.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile creams have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with hot flashes, providing relief from skin irritation.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile in creams can help ease muscle spasms and cramping, which often accompany hot flashes, reducing their severity and duration.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that may exacerbate hot flashes, promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving hot flash symptoms.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and other bioactive compounds, they have been traditionally used to calm nervousness and reduce sweating associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered chamomile with 20% beeswax and 80% sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 160-170°f for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture frequently until the wax is fully incorporated and the temperature reaches 110-120°f within 10-15 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool to room temperature with minimal stirring over 60-90 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer or whisk for 2-3 minutes then transfer it to sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often at the root of hot flashes. The cream's active ingredients, including Chinese angelica and red ginseng, work together to cool the body from within, reducing feelings of heat and sweating. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from hot flash-related discomforts such as dryness and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Reishi mushroom extract: Helps to reduce hot flashes by soothing the body's "fire" or stress response, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Dong quai (Angelica sinensis): Relieves hot flashes by balancing hormonal fluctuations, particularly estrogen levels, which can help stabilize mood and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
Licorice root: Soothes the body's "fire" or inflammation, reducing the intensity of hot flashes and night sweats while also calming digestive discomfort common in menopausal women.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm hot flashes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide relief from hot flash symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit powder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove impurities.
Combine the sifted powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Add 50 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully melted, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 10 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator at 40°f to 45°f for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with hot flashes because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as damascena oil and beta-sitosterol, work together to calm the body's response to hormonal fluctuations, reducing the severity and frequency of hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing and nourishing qualities help to soothe skin irritation and discomfort often associated with hot flashes, providing a sense of relief and comfort to those experiencing them.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Damask rose contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to hot flashes by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Estrogen-balancing properties: The estrogen-like compounds present in damask rose, such as phytoestrogens, can help regulate hormonal imbalances that cause hot flashes by providing a gentle and natural boost to estrogen levels.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties: Damask rose contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and muscle spasms associated with hot flashes, providing relief from discomfort and flushing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm hot flash symptoms.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce heat and discomfort associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosa damascena flowers weighing approximately 20 grams for infusion.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong floral extract.
Combine the cooled floral extract with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax pellets.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat stirring frequently until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set before storing in a dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a potent extract of Echinacea purpurea, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate menopausal symptoms. The cream works by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, which can help to alleviate the discomfort and heat associated with hot flashes. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of purple coneflower may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes, providing relief from these unpredictable and uncomfortable episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Herbal purple coneflower creams may help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation, which can contribute to heat intolerance and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in purple coneflower cream may help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hot flashes, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being.

Estrogen-mimicking activity: Some herbal extracts in purple coneflower creams, such as isoflavones, may mimic the effects of estrogen to help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with menopause, including hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of purple coneflower contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to help reduce symptoms of hot flashes.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which can help to alleviate the inflammatory response associated with hot flashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to extract the bioactive compounds from the herbs.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Whip the reserved liquid with 100 milliliters of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter until smooth and creamy, preparing the final product for packaging and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a combination of natural ingredients that help to calm and soothe the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion root and leaf work together to reduce heat and alleviate symptoms such as sweating, flushing, and chills. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate and cool the skin, providing relief from hot flash episodes. By addressing these physical manifestations of menopause, herbal dandelion creams can offer a natural and effective solution for managing hot flashes.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion cream's diuretic properties can help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing fluid retention, which can contribute to feelings of warmth and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion cream may help soothe and calm the body's response to hot flashes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with this symptom.

Hormone-balancing properties: Dandelion root has been traditionally used to support hormone balance and regulate menstrual cycles, which can help alleviate hot flashes by addressing underlying hormonal imbalances that may be contributing to these symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, dandelion roots help reduce hot flash symptoms by soothing and calming the body.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits contain valuable antioxidants and phytoestrogens, which help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh taraxacum officinale leaves measuring 100g for infusion.
Combine gathered leaves with 500ml of boiling distilled water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and discard solids after 10 minutes.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200g of cold-pressed organic coconut oil and 20g of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 45 minutes until melted and blended thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hot flashes because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that soothes the skin and calms the body. Curcumin's ability to reduce inflammation and modulate hormonal imbalances makes it an effective remedy for alleviating hot flash symptoms such as sweating, flushing, and rapid heartbeat. By applying turmeric creams directly to the skin, women can experience quick relief from hot flashes and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flash symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Vasodilatory effects: Turmeric creams containing vasodilators like curcumin can help dilate blood vessels, which can lead to improved circulation and reduced heat flux associated with hot flashes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin C and E, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hot flash symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the turmeric plant are rich in essential oils, including turmerone, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease hot flash discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric roots and mix with 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens and reaches 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to 120°f, then add 1 teaspoon of beeswax pellets and stir until melted.
Gradually add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and let set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hot flashes are topical treatments that help alleviate symptoms of hot flashes by applying natural compounds to the skin. They work by regulating body temperature and hormone levels, providing relief from sudden heat surges. The best herbal creams for hot flashes are Cimicifuga racemosa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Valeriana officinalis, Vitex agnus-castus, Angelica sinensis, Zingiber officinale, Paeonia lactiflora, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, hot flashes can disrupt daily routines, cause sleep disturbances, and negatively impact relationships, leading to decreased productivity and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hot flashes that are treated with herbal creams are menopausal symptoms, night sweats, and skin irritations such as eczema and acne. Herbal creams containing ingredients like dong quai, black cohosh, and evening primrose oil can help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, cooling the skin, and regulating hormones. These natural remedies offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing hot flash-like conditions, promoting overall wellness and comfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hot flashes is a blend of Peppermint, Calendula, and Sage. Peppermint helps to cool the body and reduce symptoms of heat, while Calendula soothes and calms the skin. Sage, rich in estrogen-mimicking compounds, helps to regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to hot flashes. This combination can be applied topically to the wrists, neck, or temples to provide fast relief from hot flashes and promote a sense of calm and well-being. Regular use can lead to reduced frequency and severity of hot flashes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy Research, Silybum marianum extract (400mg/d) was found to be effective in reducing hot flashes in women. The study involved 80 women who were randomly allocated into two groups: one receiving Silybum marianum extract and the other receiving a placebo. The results showed that hot flashes frequency and severity decreased significantly in the Silybum marianum group, from 4.32 ± 0.20/day and 5.25 ± 0.22 to 1.31 ± 0.15/day and 1.62 ± 0.08, respectively, after 12 weeks (p < 0.001), indicating the potential benefits of herbal creams in managing hot flashes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams helps with hot flashes because they contain a unique blend of compounds that work to regulate the body's hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause. The active ingredients in these creams, such as sesquiterpenes and lignans, have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to reduce hot flash frequency and intensity. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, Cimicifuga racemosa creams can also help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to hot flashes.

Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with hot flashes because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extract interacts with the body's hormonal system, regulating cortisol levels and reducing the impact of hormonal fluctuations associated with menopause. The cooling and soothing effects of the cream can also help alleviate the physical discomfort and sweating associated with hot flashes. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote relaxation, helping to manage symptoms and improve overall well-being.

Valeriana officinalis creams helps with hot flashes because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that has a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. The anti-inflammatory properties of valerian root may also contribute to its hot flash relief benefits by reducing stress and anxiety, which can trigger hot flashes. Additionally, valerian root has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, further aiding in hot flash management.

Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with hot flashes because they target the root cause of menopausal symptoms, which is hormonal imbalance. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and iridoids, interact with the body's hormone system, promoting a balance of estrogen and progesterone levels. This balance leads to a decrease in hot flash frequency and severity, providing relief to women experiencing menopausal symptoms. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus creams can also help alleviate other symptoms associated with menopause, such as mood swings and night sweats.

Angelica sinensis creams helps with hot flashes because they contain natural compounds that regulate hormonal balance and circulation. The active ingredients, including ferulic acid and isoflavones, have been shown to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in menopausal women. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, these creams also alleviate associated symptoms such as night sweats and flushing. Furthermore, the anti-oxidant properties of Angelica sinensis help to protect against oxidative stress, contributing to its effectiveness in managing hot flashes.

Zingiber officinale creams helps with hot flashes because they contain ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory and thermogenic agent. Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery to the skin, where it can be absorbed and work to calm the nervous system and reduce blood flow to the surface of the skin, providing relief from hot flashes and night sweats. This natural approach can be a valuable addition to other hot flash treatments.

Paeonia lactiflora creams helps with hot flashes because they contain the root of the peony plant, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to balance and regulate the body's energy. The creams are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which are often triggers for hot flashes. By soothing the skin and reducing inflammation, Paeonia lactiflora creams can provide relief from hot flashes and promote overall well-being.

Avena sativa creams helps with hot flashes because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help regulate the body's temperature and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes. The herb Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, is believed to have a balancing effect on hormones, particularly estrogen, which is often imbalanced in women experiencing hot flashes. By applying Avena sativa creams topically, the active compounds can be absorbed through the skin, providing relief and reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

Hypericum perforatum creams helps with hot flashes because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on hormone balance and neurotransmitter regulation. This helps to alleviate symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as sweating, flushing, and mood changes. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Hypericum perforatum creams may also contribute to their therapeutic benefits, promoting overall well-being and reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. This natural remedy may provide a safe and effective alternative for managing hot flashes.

Angelica archangelica creams helps with hot flashes because of its ability to regulate the body's hormonal balance and reduce inflammation. The cream's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and vitamin B6, help to stabilize the body's temperature and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes. Additionally, the cream's cooling and soothing properties provide instant relief from the discomfort associated with hot flashes, promoting relaxation and a sense of calm. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for managing hot flash symptoms.
